Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Block at rows 35-39, cols 34-37
# Check if UP is possible from this position
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("=== Above block (rows 25-35, cols 33-40) ===")
for i in range(25, 36):
    segment = ascii_lines[i][33:40]
    print(f"  Row {i:2d}: '{segment}'")

# Also check DOWN
print("\n=== Below block (rows 39-50, cols 33-40) ===")
for i in range(39, 50):
    segment = ascii_lines[i][33:40]
    print(f"  Row {i:2d}: '{segment}'")

# Try moving UP from current position
result = action(['UP'])
print(f"\nAfter UP: done={last_action_result.get('done')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"  Orange: boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] >= 10:
        print(f"  Blue block: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
